title: Light from the Number Line: Complete Analysis

polarization.source: Pythagorean triples → rational points on S¹
polarization.completeness: By equidistribution of Farey fractions, these angles become dense in [0, 2π), encoding ALL possible polarization states.

diffraction.interpretation: r₂(n) counts lattice points on the circle of radius √n. In a square-lattice diffraction experiment, this gives the intensity at each diffraction order.

beam_splitting.pattern: Primes ≡ 1 (mod 4) split light into two modes. Primes ≡ 3 (mod 4) transmit without splitting. The prime 2 couples both polarizations.

wave_equation.source: Pythagorean relation as discrete wave equation
wave_equation.key_insight: a² + b² = c² is the spatial wave equation on ℤ. The multiplicative closure under Brahmagupta-Fibonacci mirrors superposition of electromagnetic waves.

quantum.connection: θ₃(q) = 1 + 2q + 2q⁴ + 2q⁹ + ... sums over perfect squares from the number line. Its square generates r₂(n), linking quantum statistics to diffraction.

interference.source: Multiple Pythagorean representations of same hypotenuse
interference.interpretation: Each distinct triple with the same c represents a coherent beam. Multiple triples → multi-beam interference. The NUMBER of representations determines fringe complexity.

spectrum.source: Distribution of Pythagorean hypotenuses on number line
spectrum.asymptotic: The density of spectral lines follows the Landau-Ramanujan theorem: ~K/√(log N)

connections.description: The distribution of "birefringent" vs "opaque" primes (p≡1 vs p≡3 mod 4) is governed by the zeros of L(s, χ₄). GRH controls the error term in the "optical prime counting function."
connections.implication: If GRH holds, the "refractive index" of the prime number sequence converges at optimal rate.

connections.description: The generating function for r₂(n) is a modular form. Modular transformations τ → -1/τ correspond to Fourier duality, which IS the wave-particle duality of light. The Langlands correspondence thus has an optical interpretation.
Rational points on S² (from Pythagorean triples) correspond to exactly-synthesizable quantum gates. The Ross-Selinger algorithm for gate synthesis is essentially finding "good" Pythagorean triples.
connections.application: More efficient quantum gate synthesis by exploiting the algebraic structure of Pythagorean triples.

connections.description: The sparsity of r₂(n) implies that "light signals" on the integer lattice are naturally compressible. Gaussian integer factorization provides a number-theoretic compression scheme analogous to DCT/FFT-based compression (JPEG, MP3).
connections.application: Novel compression algorithms based on Gaussian integer factorization of signal energy.

connections.description: Neural network geometry is fundamentally Pythagorean. The light-from-numbers framework suggests new quantization, initialization, and optimization strategies based on the arithmetic of sums of squares.
applications: Gaussian integer arithmetic for complex networks
